choice: Commit every valid row and return a per-row report of what was rejected and why (unparseable value, unknown identifier, unresolved account), letting the user fix and re-upload only the failures. Treat the import as additive and non-destructive — it only adds observations, never zeroes or deletes existing ones.
rationale: This matches the fail-closed, never-destroy posture already set for sync (b45b7691) and the keep-every-row guarantee (777f97e5): a CSV import is just another observation source, so a few bad rows shouldn't block good data, and no import should ever clobber existing balances. A clear rejection report is the import analogue of sync's stale/last-success surfacing.
